publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-03-09 23:46:49 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, notes, calculations, materials for talks, data collection information, publication drafts and reprints, and course materials.
did.abstract: Includes research and teaching materials of Barbara Hope Cooper, a Cornell University physics professor.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Barbara Hope Cooper, 1953-1999, Cornell Class of 1976, graduated Magna Cum Laude in Physics and with Distinction in all Subjects, with a B.A. degree in Physics; then attended graduate school at the California Institute of Technology until June 1982 when she received a Ph.D. in Physics. She returned to Cornell as an Assistant Professor of Physics in 1983, became an Associate Professor of Physics at Cornell in 1989, and a full Professor in 1995.
Her main research interests as an experimental physicist and a leader in surface science were:
1) the use of low-energy ion beams to probe particle-surface interactions and the structural and electronic features of clean and adsorbate-covered metal surfaces,
2) atomic-scale scanning tunneling microscopy studies of mass transport and stability of nanoscale structures on ion-bombarded metal surfaces,
3) in situ studies of surface roughening during bombardment using synchrotron x-rays.
She guided a dozen students through their doctorates at Cornell.

She worked for Bell Laboratories, Kellogg Radiation Laboratory at Cal Tech, and Cornell University. She was awarded an Avon Scholarship, 1971-1976; a Bell Laboratories Graduate Research Program for Women Grant, 1976-1981; a Presidential Young Investigator Award, 1985-1989, an IBM Faculty Development Award, 1985 and 1986; and the Maria Goeppert-Mayer Award of the American Physical Society, 1992. She was a member of the American Vacuum Society, Sigma Xi, Bohemian Physical Society and a fellow of the American Physical Society.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The collection includes correspondence, notes, calculations, materials for talks, data collection information, publication drafts and reprints, and course materials from student years as well as outlines, exams, and lab manuals used in teaching.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Barbara Hope Cooper Papers, #14-22-3072. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to Box 13 restricted to the permission of the University Archivist.

did.unittitle: Sigma Xi membership certificate, Cal Tech
APS: March Meeting, 1992/CSWP panel discussion (Women Physicists)
APS: DCMP (Division of Condensed Matter Physics) 1997-98
Funding: Congress - DOD (Department of Defense) funding cuts 1994
Presidential Young Investigators Awards, National Science Foundation
Job Application Documents: CV, Reprints, etc.
Notes: Research discussions, seminars, AT & T Trip, Univ. VA, Gordon - Particle Solid - 1990
Notes: Mar. 16, 1992 - Apr. 22, 1992, also March Meeting 1992, Apr. 10, 1992 - CEX experiments to be done
Gordon Research Conference [Georgetown]: Atomic Collisions in Solids, "Gas Surface Dynamic," Colby-Sawyer
Gordon Research Conference: Dynamics at Surfaces, Aug. 6-11, 1995
Gordon Research Conference: "Materials Processes Far From Equilibrium" (viewgraph originals)
FCP (Fractionally Charged Particles) talk to PH 10 12/7/82
MSC: 1 yr + 3 yr proposal/surface group summary
